info.name: LlamaGen.ai – Internal API (Full)
info.description:
# LlamaGen.ai Internal API Collection

Comprehensive Postman collection for the LlamaGen.ai **Internal Web API** — the same API used by the LlamaGen web application.

## Setup Instructions

1. **Import this collection** into Postman
    
2. **Import the environment** file `LlamaGen_API.postman_environment.json`
    
3. **Set your session token:**
    
    - Log into [https://llamagen.ai](https://llamagen.ai)
        
    - Open DevTools (F12) → Application → Cookies → `llamagen.ai`
        
    - Copy the value of `__Secure-next-auth.session-token`
        
    - Paste it into the `session_token` environment variable
        
4. **Set your user ID** (found in any `/detail` response under `userId`)
    
5. Select the **LlamaGen API** environment in Postman
    
6. Start with **"Auth → Check Session"** to verify your token works
    

## Auto-Refresh Token

The collection includes a **pre-request script** that automatically checks session validity before each request. If the session has expired, it attempts to refresh via NextAuth's session endpoint. If refresh fails, you'll get a clear error message to manually update the token.

## API Architecture

| Aspect | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Base URL | `https://llamagen.ai/api` |
| Auth | Session Cookie |
| Content-Type | `multipart/form-data` (POST) |
| Parameters | 30+ |
| Plan | App Pro ($24/mo, 45,000 credits) |
| Cost | 150 credits per panel |

## Folder Structure

- **Auth** – Session management & auto-refresh
    
- **Characters** – Upload photos, create characters, manage comicRoles
    
- **Comics** – Full comic generation with all 30+ parameters
    
- **Polling & Results** – Track generation progress and retrieve results
    
- **Multi-Page** – Continue writing to build multi-page comics
    
- **Public API (Fallback)** – Limited 3-parameter public API
    

## Important Notes

- All POST parameters are sent as `multipart/form-data` strings
    
- Booleans are `"true"` / `"false"` (strings)
    
- Arrays/objects are JSON-encoded strings
    
- The `_t` / `t` query param is a cache-buster (Unix timestamp in ms)
    
- Characters are referenced in prompts by their `actor`/`name` field

request.description:
Checks if the current session token is still valid.

**Expected response (valid):**
```json
{
  "user": {
    "name": "Your Name",
    "email": "your@email.com",

    "id": "cmlqhsijp004cl4049qam4te8"
  },
  "expires": "2026-03-23T..."
}
```

**Expected response (expired):**
```json
{}
```

request.description:
Attempts to re-authenticate using email/password credentials.

**Prerequisites:**
1. Run **Get CSRF Token** first
2. Set `login_email` and `login_password` in environment variables

**Note:** This only works if LlamaGen uses email/password authentication (credentials provider). If they use OAuth (Google, GitHub, etc.), you'll need to extract the session token from the browser manually.

The test script automatically extracts and stores the new session token from the `Set-Cookie` header.

Lists all authentication providers configured in LlamaGen's NextAuth.js. Run this FIRST to check if 'credentials' provider exists. If only OAuth (google, github) is listed, programmatic login from Postman is not possible — you must extract the session cookie from the browser manually.

Sends a magic link sign-in email. No password needed — LlamaGen uses passwordless email auth.

Prerequisites:
1. Run 'Get CSRF Token' first
2. Set login_email in environment

After sending:
1. Check your email inbox
2. Copy the full magic link URL
3. Paste it into 'Email Sign-In (Complete)' request

item.description:
Session management for the Internal API. LlamaGen uses **NextAuth.js** for authentication.

**Session Cookie:** `__Secure-next-auth.session-token`

**Auto-refresh:** The collection-level pre-request script checks session validity every 30 minutes and attempts auto-refresh. If the session is fully expired (e.g., after days of inactivity), you'll need to manually re-extract the cookie from your browser.

**How to get the token:**

1. Log into [https://llamagen.ai](https://llamagen.ai)
    
2. DevTools (F12) → Application → Cookies → llamagen.ai
    
3. Copy `__Secure-next-auth.session-token` value
    
4. Paste into the `session_token` environment variable

request.description:
Gets an S3 presigned URL for uploading a character reference image.

**Request body:**

- `fileName` — The filename (e.g., `character_photo.jpg`)
    
- `fileType` — MIME type (`image/jpeg`, `image/png`)
    
- `filePath` — Upload path, usually `u/{userId}/`
    

**After this request:**  
The presigned URL is auto-stored in `{{presigned_url}}`. Use it in step 2 to upload the actual image.

**Final image URL format:**  
`https://s.llamagen.ai/u/{userId}/{fileName}`

Uploads the character reference image to S3 using the presigned URL from step 1.

**How to use:**

1. In the Body tab, select **Binary**
    
2. Click **Select File** and choose your character photo
    
3. Ensure the `Content-Type` header matches your image type
    

**Supported formats:** JPEG, PNG

**After upload, the image is accessible at:**  
`https://s.llamagen.ai/u/{{user_id}}/character_photo.jpg`

formdata.description:
Full character attributes JSON. Fields:
- gender: female | male
- ethnicity: White, Asian, Black, Hispanic, etc.
- age: numeric string
- bodyType: Normal, Slim, Athletic, Heavy
- hairStyle: long, short, curly, straight, bob, etc. (20+ base + 56 more)
- hairColor: pink, blonde, brown, black, red, etc. (20+ base + 31 more)
- dress: Custom text or preset (20 base + 58 more)
- style: photo | avatar | anime
- image: S3 URL of uploaded reference photo
- expression: Happy, Sad, Angry, Surprised, Curious, etc. (20+ options)
- equipment: Custom text or preset (19 base + 112 more)
- viewAngle: halfBody | fullBody
- characterDisplay: front | side | back
- characterType: Human | Dog | Cat | etc.
- characterPrompt: Custom generation override

request.description:
Creates a new character from a reference photo and attribute definitions.

**Character Types:**
| Type | Pro Required |
|------|-------------|
| Realistic (photo) | No |
| Avatar | No |
| Anime | Yes |
| Animal | No |
| Multi-Character | Yes |
| Sketch-Draw | Yes |
| Story Cast | No |

**Prompt auto-format:**
`((masterpiece,best quality)){style}, one {gender}, {ethnicity}, {age} years old, {bodyType} bodytype`

**After creation:** The character data (with AI-generated descriptions) can be retrieved via the Detail endpoint and used in `comicRoles[]` for comic generation.

request.description:
Retrieves the full detail of a created character, including AI-generated descriptions.

The response contains the character data that should be used in the `comicRoles[]` array when generating comics.

**Key fields in response:**
- `comicRoles` — Array with the character definition
- `status` — `LOADING` or `PROCESSED`
- Character's `fullDescription`, `shortDescription`

item.description:
Character management — upload photos, create AI characters, and retrieve character data for use in `comicRoles[]`.

**Workflow:**
1. **Get Presigned URL** → S3 
            upload URL for the photo
2. **Upload Image** → PUT the photo binary to S3
3. **Create Character** → POST to `/api/artworks` with `action=createCharacter`
4. **Use in Comics** → 
            Include the character in the `comicRoles` array when generating comics

**Character Types:** Realistic, Avatar, Anime (Pro), Animal, Multi-Character (Pro), Sketch-Draw (Pro), Story 
            Cast

**Characters are referenced by name in prompts:** e.g., "Elena plays soccer in the park"

request.description:
Creates a new comic with **all 30+ parameters**. This is the primary generation endpoint.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "code": 200,
  "artwork": { "count": 1 },
  "consumedCredits": 300,
  "prediectGeneratedPanelsCount": 2
}
```

**Cost:** 150 credits × number of panels

**After submission:**
1. Note the artwork ID from the response
2. Use **Polling → Get Progress** to track generation stages
3. Use **Polling → Get Detail** to retrieve finished panels

**Generation pipeline:**
1. Processing story instruction (40%) — GPT-4.1
2. Creating storyboard layout
3. Creating script content (60%)
4. Generated N images — Imagen 4.0
5. Completing (100%)

**Total time:** ~90s for 2 panels, ~120s for 4 panels

request.description:
Creates a comic featuring **custom characters** from the `comicRoles` array.

**How characters work:**
1. Characters are defined in the `comicRoles` JSON array
2. Each character has an `actor`/`name` field
3. Reference characters in the `prompt` by their actor name
4. The AI matches the name to the character definition and applies visual attributes

**Example:** If `comicRoles` contains a character named "Elena", the prompt "Elena plays soccer" will render Elena with her defined appearance.

**fullDescription pattern:**
`{Name}({age} years old,{gender},{characterType},{ethnicity} ethnicity,{dress},{hair details},no glasses,{skin} skin,{bodyType})`

**Important:** Replace the example `comicRoles` values with your actual character data from the Character creation step.

3. Crossing a rope bridge
4. Arriving at dragon's lair

**Key field:** `comicData[].panels[].prompt`
- Empty string = AI generates the scene from the overall story
- Filled string = Exact scene description for that specific panel

**Cost:** 600 credits (4 × 150)

item.description:
Comic generation with full control over all 30+ parameters.

**Cost:** 150 credits per panel

**Generation time:** ~90s for 2 panels, ~120s for 4 panels

**Styles:** 500+ presets available (confirmed: `comedyComicBook2`, `animeFrame2`)

**Panel counts:** 1–40 panels per prompt (Pro plan)

**After submission:** Use the Polling folder to track progress and retrieve results.

item.name: Polling & Results

request.description:
Gets real-time generation progress.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "stage": "Creating script content",
  "progress": 60,
  "updatedAt": 1771665492433,
  "stageProgress": 0,
  "stageDuration": 20000,
  "generatedImages": 2,
  "extraData": { "pageIndex": 0, "panelIndex": 1 }
}
```

**Stages in order:**
1. `Processing story instruction` (40%)
2. `Creating storyboard layout`
3. `Creating script content` (60%)
4. `Generated N images`
5. `Completing` (100%)

**Poll interval:** ~6 seconds

exec: console.log('✅ Generation complete! Use Get Detail to retrieve results.');
console.log('⏳ Still generating... Check progress for more detail.');

request.description:
Quick status check — lightweight endpoint that returns only the current status.

**Response:**
```json
{ "status": "LOADING" }
```
or
```json
{ "status": "PROCESSED" }
```

**Poll interval:** ~12 seconds

request.description:
Gets the **full result** including all panel images, captions, character data, and pipeline configuration.

**Response fields (when PROCESSED):**
- `id` — Artwork ID
- `status` — `PROCESSED`
- `comicData` — Panel array with `assetUrl` (image URLs), `caption` text
- `comicRoles` — Character definitions (including AI-generated descriptions)
- `comicLocations` — Auto-generated location data
- `generator_spec` — Backend pipeline config (model versions, settings)
- `templatePrompt` — Style prompt template for the preset
- `version` — Current version number (needed for continueWrite)
- `imageUrl` — Primary panel image URL
- `font`, `fontSize`, `lang`, `preset`, `layout` — Settings used

**The test script automatically:**
- Parses and logs all panel image URLs and captions
- Stores the `version` number for multi-page continuation
- Logs the generator spec (model versions)

item.description:
Track comic generation progress and retrieve finished results.

**Polling pattern (from HAR analysis of 72 requests):**
- `/progress` → every ~6 seconds
- `/status` → every ~12 seconds
- `/detail` → every ~12 seconds

**Generation stages:**
1. Processing story instruction (40%)
2. Creating storyboard layout
3. Creating script content (60%)
4. Generated N images
5. Completing (100%)

**Status values:** `LOADING` → `PROCESSED`

The `_t` / `t` query param is a cache-buster (Unix timestamp in ms). Postman's `{{$timestamp}}` generates this automatically.

request.description:
Adds a new page to an existing comic.

**Multi-page comic building:**
```
Request 1: POST /api/artworks (no action) → Creates comic with page 0
Request 2: POST /api/artworks (action=continueWrite) → Adds page 1
Request 3: POST /api/artworks (action=continueWrite) → Adds page 2
...
```

**Required fields for continueWrite:**
- `action` = `continueWrite`
- `id` = existing artwork ID
- `currentVersion` = incremented version (auto-incremented by pre-request script)
- `comicRoles` = full character array from original comic

**Important:** The `page` index in `comicData` must be incremented for each new page (0, 1, 2, 3...).

The pre-request script automatically increments `current_version`.

item.description:
Add additional pages to an existing comic using the `continueWrite` action.

**Workflow:**
1. Create initial comic → Get artwork ID and version
2. `continueWrite` with the same ID → Adds page 1
3. `continueWrite` again → Adds page 2
4. Repeat for as many pages as needed

**Important:** Each `continueWrite` must include:
- `id` — The existing artwork ID
- `currentVersion` — Incremented version number
- `comicRoles` — Full character array (for consistency)
- `action` — Must be `continueWrite`

item.name: Public API (Fallback)

request.description:
Generates a comic via the Public REST API.

**Only 3 parameters work:**
- `model` — `cyani-model` (required)
- `prompt` — Story description
- `size` — `1024x1024`

**Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "cm20e3dnb00097k8753vd0wt4",
  "status": "LOADING",
  "prompt": "...",
  "usage": { "total_comics": 4 },
  "model": "cyani-model"
}
```

**Always returns:** 4 panels, no captions, English storyboard.

item.description:
The Public REST API at `https://api.llamagen.ai/v1`.

**Limitations confirmed:** Only 3 parameters work (`model`, `prompt`, `size`). All others are silently ignored. Always returns 4 panels with English storyboard, no captions, no dialogs.

**Auth:** Bearer Token (API key from Settings → API tab)

**Cost:** Separate API Plan required ($100/mo for Pro)

**Recommendation:** Use Internal API instead. This folder is for fallback / reference only.
